name: Sibiu
summary: Sibiu is one of Romania's safest cities with very low crime rates and excellent walkability. The medieval town center is well-maintained and tourist-friendly, with friendly locals and reliable infrastructure. Romania maintains its Level 1 travel advisory from the US State Department, indicating normal precautions are sufficient.
quickVerdict: Sibiu ranks among the safest destinations in Romania and Eastern Europe, with crime levels significantly lower than many Western European cities. The city is particularly safe for solo travelers including women, with a well-lit historic center and helpful locals. While petty theft can occur in tourist areas, violent crime is extremely rare. The main safety considerations are basic precautions against pickpockets and potential earthquake risk, though seismic activity is generally mild in this region.

neighborhoods.name: Historic Center (Piata Mare)
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Sibiu with cobblestone streets, cafes, and major attractions. Well-patrolled and well-lit, perfect for walking day and night. Some tourist-targeted petty crime possible but very safe overall.

neighborhoods.name: Lower Town (Orasul de Jos)
neighborhoods.description: Traditional residential area below the upper town. Quiet residential streets with good lighting and regular foot traffic. Very safe for tourists exploring local neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.name: Dumbravii District
neighborhoods.description: Modern residential area with shopping centers and parks. Well-developed infrastructure but less tourist presence. Safe but may feel isolated after dark for some visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Zone
neighborhoods.description: Manufacturing and industrial area on city outskirts. Limited tourist attractions and reduced police presence. Generally safe during day but avoid at night unless necessary.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Transportation hub with moderate activity levels. Some petty crime reports and homeless populations. Exercise normal precautions, especially with luggage and late-night arrivals.

neighborhoods.name: Suburbs and Villages
neighborhoods.description: Rural villages and suburbs surrounding Sibiu. Extremely low crime rates and friendly communities. Very safe but limited services and police response times may be longer.

scams.name: ATM Skimming
scams.description: Fraudulent devices attached to ATMs to steal card information, particularly targeting tourist areas and airport locations.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks, check for tampering before use, cover your PIN entry, and avoid standalone machines in isolated areas.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Airport and tourist area taxis quoting inflated fixed prices or claiming broken meters to overcharge tourists, especially from Sibiu airport.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber or Bolt apps, insist on meter usage, agree on fare beforehand, or use official taxi companies with visible rates (942 or 953).

scams.name: Restaurant Bill Padding
scams.description: Restaurants accidentally adding extra items or higher-priced items to tourist bills, particularly wine or drinks not ordered.
scams.howToAvoid: Check bills carefully before payment, keep receipts, and politely question discrepancies. Learn basic Romanian numbers to verify charges.

scams.name: Fake Police Officers
scams.description: Individuals posing as plainclothes police asking to check wallets for counterfeit money or drugs.
scams.howToAvoid: Ask for proper identification, never hand over your wallet, ask to go to nearest station if suspicious, and call 112 to verify if needed.

soloFemale.overview: Sibiu is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, ranking among the safest cities in Romania. Women report feeling comfortable walking alone day and night in the city center. Harassment is rare, and locals are generally helpful and respectful. The compact old town is well-lit and heavily trafficked by tourists and locals.
tips: Stay in central accommodations near Piata Mare for best safety and convenience
Avoid walking alone in poorly lit areas outside the historic center after dark
Keep emergency number 112 accessible and inform someone of your itinerary
Use reputable accommodation with good reviews and reception staff presence
Trust your instincts and don't hesitate to seek help from local authorities if needed

nightSafety.overview: Sibiu's historic center remains safe and well-lit throughout the night with regular foot traffic until late evening. Street lighting is adequate and police presence is visible in tourist areas. The city has a relaxed nightlife scene with most venues closing by 2 AM, creating a safe environment for evening activities.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas when walking at night
Use official taxis or ride-sharing apps rather than walking long distances
Avoid excessive alcohol consumption and don't leave drinks unattended
Travel in groups when possible and keep valuables secure
Be aware that nightlife options are limited compared to larger cities

transport.metro: Sibiu does not have a metro system
transport.rideshare: Uber and Bolt operate in Sibiu with good coverage and competitive pricing. Generally reliable and safer than street taxis, especially for airport transfers where rates are transparent
transport.taxis: Local taxis with companies 942 and 953 are legitimate. Always ensure the meter is running and rates are displayed. Airport taxis may overcharge tourists, so negotiate or use apps instead
transport.tips: Public buses run by Tursib are safe and efficient for local transport. Walking is the best option in the historic center. For longer distances, ride-sharing apps offer better value and safety than street taxis.

customs: Romanians appreciate polite greetings and handshakes with eye contact
Dress modestly when visiting churches and religious sites
Tipping 10% is customary in restaurants for good service
Bringing small gifts like flowers or chocolates when visiting homes is appreciated
Learning basic Romanian phrases like 'multumesc' (thank you) is well-received

health.overview: Healthcare in Sibiu is adequate with both public and private facilities available. The city has modern hospitals and clinics, though private healthcare offers better service and English-speaking staff. EU citizens can use European Health Insurance Cards for emergency treatment.
health.water: Tap water in Sibiu is generally safe to drink and meets EU standards. Bottled water is widely available if preferred, especially for those with sensitive stomachs
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond routine immunizations. Tick-borne encephalitis vaccination recommended for extensive outdoor activities in rural areas
health.altitude: Sibiu sits at 400m elevation with no altitude concerns. The city has a continental climate with cold winters and warm summers. Air quality is generally good with minimal pollution

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Bucharest: +40-21-200-3300 (general), +40-21-270-6000 (emergencies), 4-6 Dr. Liviu Librescu Blvd., District 1, Bucharest

faq.a: Yes, Sibiu is very safe for tourists and ranks among Romania's safest cities. Crime rates are low, locals are friendly, and the historic center is well-maintained with good lighting and police presence. Basic precautions are sufficient for a safe visit.

Yes, Sibiu is generally safe at night, especially in the historic center which remains well-lit and populated until late evening. However, stick to main streets and avoid poorly lit areas outside the center. The city has a calm nightlife scene with most venues closing by 2 AM.

faq.q: Is Sibiu safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Absolutely. Sibiu is considered one of the safest cities in Romania for solo female travelers. Women report feeling comfortable walking alone day and night in the city center. Harassment is rare, and locals are generally respectful and helpful to visitors.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Sibiu?
faq.a: Sibiu has no particularly dangerous areas, but exercise normal caution in the industrial zones on city outskirts and around the train station at night. The entire historic center and main residential areas are very safe for tourists at all times.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Sibiu?
faq.a: Yes, public transport in Sibiu is safe and reliable. The Tursib bus system is efficient and well-maintained. For taxis, use reputable companies (942 or 953) or ride-sharing apps like Uber and Bolt, which offer transparent pricing and better safety standards than street taxis.
